title: oxytropis leucantha var. depressa
intro: Oxytropis leucantha var. depressa, also known as White Locoweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of White Locoweed are attributed to its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its roots and leaves can be used to create poultices for topical applications.
